system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(
    ( 0  0   0)    // 0
    (20  0   0)    // 1
    (20  20  0)    // 2
    ( 0  20  0)    // 3
    ( 0  0   1)    // 4
    (20  0   1)    // 5
    (20  20  1)    // 6
    ( 0  20  1)    // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    air
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='chemistryProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      chemistryProperties;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

chemistryType
{
    solver          ode;
    method          standard;
}

chemistry       on;

initialChemicalTimeStep 1e-07;

odeCoeffs
{
    solver          seulex;
    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}

}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='reactions', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2

reactions

        type        irreversibleArrheniusReaction;

        beta        0;
        Ta          24358;
    }
}

keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/2/constant/physicalProperties"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/2/constant/physicalProperties from line 12 to line 30.

<review_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This is the same error as Attempt 4, which means the previous fix of embedding species sub-dictionaries directly in `physicalProperties` didn't work, OR the approach of putting species in separate files in `constant/` also didn't work because the solver still can't find `CH4`.

Let me reconsider the OpenFOAM 10 architecture. In OpenFOAM 10 with `hePsiThermo` and `multiComponentMixture`, the species property files are looked up in `constant/` **by name**, but the solver may be reading them differently. Looking more carefully at the reference tutorial structure and the actual error, I believe the issue is that the species property files need to be discovered via the `reactions` file mechanism, not just by having a `species` list in `physicalProperties`.

## Root Cause

In OpenFOAM 10, the `multiComponentMixture` constructs itself from the `species` list in `physicalProperties`, BUT the individual species property files must be accessible. The problem from the previous attempt was likely that the solver tried to interpret the inline sub-dictionaries as files. 

However, looking at the current state again, the real issue might be simpler: the `physicalProperties` file currently has **both** the old-style chemistry reader directives AND the inline species sub-dictionaries, creating confusion. Let me check what the current file actually contains.

Wait — reviewing the current files provided, I see that `physicalProperties` already has the `species` list and inline species sub-dictionaries. But the error says `CH4` is undefined in `physicalProperties`. This suggests that even though the entries are there, the parser isn't finding them.

Actually, re-reading the current `physicalProperties` carefully, I notice it has `inertSpecie N2;` but the species list includes `CH4`, `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, `CO2`. The `inertSpecie` declaration alone may not be enough — OpenFOAM 10 may require ALL species to be declared as `specie` entries or included differently.

## Solution

The approach from Attempt 4 was essentially correct, but let me ensure it's done cleanly without any legacy entries interfering. Here's the precise fix:

### 1. Clean `constant/physicalProperties`

Remove ONLY the two legacy chemistry reader directives:
- Remove `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;`
- Remove `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";`
- Remove `foamChemistryThermoFile "<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas";`

Keep everything else intact: the `thermoType` with `multiComponentMixture`, the `inertSpecie N2;`, the `species` list, and the inline species sub-dictionaries (`CH4 { MW 16.043; }`, `O2 { MW 31.998; }`, etc.).

### 2. Verify `reactions` file format

Ensure the `constant/reactions` file has the proper OpenFOAM 10 format. It should contain:
```
species
(
    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2
);

reactions
{
    methaneReaction
    {
        type        irreversibleArrheniusReaction;
        reaction    "CH4 + 2O2 = CO2 + 2H2O";
        A           5.012e11;
        beta        0;
        Ta          24358;
    }
}
```

### 3. Verify `thermo.compressibleGas`

If this file exists in `constant/`, it should be kept. But if it conflicts, it can be removed since the species properties are now defined inline in `physicalProperties`.

### Key Point

The critical fix from the last attempt that failed was removing the legacy chemistry reader directives. Everything else (keeping `multiComponentMixture`, keeping the `species` list, keeping inline species sub-dictionaries with `MW`) should remain. The `inertSpecie N2;` entry is fine — it just declares N2 as inert; the species list provides the full composition.

After ensuring `physicalProperties` has clean `multiComponentMixture` configuration with `species` list and inline species MW entries (and no conflicting legacy entries), the `CH4` keyword should be found.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.2 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/physicalProperties', changes='Remove the three legacy chemistry reader directives: Remove chemistryReader foamChemistryReader; Remove foamChemistryFile entry; Remove foamChemistryThermoFile entry; Keep all other entries intact including thermoType with multiComponentMixture, inertSpecie N2, the species list, and inline species sub-dictionaries with MW values')]
